# Zeus

ZEUS is a mobile Bitcoin and Lightning Network wallet and remote node manager, built with TypeScript and React Native for Android and iOS.

## Repository Structure

- `App.tsx` — Application entry point
- `NavigationService.ts` — Navigation configuration
- `views/` — Screen components (64 screens: Activity, Channels, Invoice, AddContact, LnurlAuth, LightningAddress, Cashu, LSPS1, LSPS7, etc.)
- `components/` — Reusable UI components (80+ components: Amount, Button, AmountInput, QR display, etc.)
- `stores/` — MobX state management stores (31 stores: BalanceStore, ChannelsStore, ActivityStore, InvoicesStore, FeeStore, FiatStore, etc.)
- `utils/` — Utility functions (97 files: AmountUtils, Bolt11Utils, AddressUtils, CashuUtils, Bip39Utils, Base64Utils, etc.)
- `backends/` — Lightning node backend implementations (LND, CLN, LNC, EmbeddedLND, LndHub, NostrWalletConnect)
- `locales/` — Internationalization files (`en.json` is the source file for all translations)
- `android/` — Android native project
- `ios/` — iOS native project
- `proto/` — gRPC/protobuf definitions for Lightning APIs

## Key Backend Implementations

- `backends/LND.ts` — LND (Lightning Network Daemon) remote connection
- `backends/CLNRest.ts` — Core Lightning (CLN) REST connection
- `backends/LNC.ts` — Lightning Node Connect (LNC) via macaroon auth
- `backends/EmbeddedLND.ts` — Embedded LND node (mobile-native)
- `backends/LndHub.ts` — LNDHub connection (e.g., BlueWallet)
- `backends/NostrWalletConnect.ts` — Nostr Wallet Connect (NWC) protocol

## Technology Stack

- TypeScript with React Native
- MobX for state management
- React Navigation for routing
- Jest for testing
- ESLint + Prettier for code quality
- Protocol Buffers (protobuf) for Lightning API communication

## Build and Development Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
yarn install

# Start development server
yarn start

# Run on Android
yarn android

# Run on iOS
cd ios && pod install && cd .. && yarn ios

# Run all checks (required before PR)
yarn verify

# Run tests
yarn test

# Type checking
yarn tsc

# Code formatting
yarn prettier          # check
yarn prettier-write    # fix

# Linting
yarn lint              # check
yarn lint-fix          # fix

# Generate protobuf types
yarn gen-proto
```

## Code Style

- TypeScript for all code; avoid `any` types
- Prettier for formatting, ESLint for linting
- Component files: PascalCase (e.g., `PaymentRequest.tsx`)
- Utility files: camelCase (e.g., `formatBalance.ts`)
- Stores: PascalCase with `Store` suffix (e.g., `ChannelsStore.ts`)
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
- Tests placed alongside code (e.g., `utils/foo.ts` and `utils/foo.test.ts`)

## Commit Message Format

```
component: Short summary (50 chars max)
```

Prefixes: `views:`, `components:`, `stores:`, `backends:`, `utils:`, `build:`, `docs:`, `tests:`

## Contribution Guidelines

- Open an issue before significant changes; work-in-progress PRs are welcome
- All PRs require `yarn verify` to pass (tests + prettier + tsc + lint)
- Only modify `locales/en.json` for translations; other locales managed via Transifex
- PR template includes platform testing checklist (iOS/Android) — do not delete it
- Security-sensitive changes (crypto operations, key management) require extra care
- License: AGPLv3

## Important Constraints

- Financial software: bugs can result in fund loss — code quality and testing are critical
- Never log sensitive data (private keys, macaroons, seeds)
- Validate all payment-related inputs
- Test UI changes on both Android and iOS
- Avoid trivial changes (typo fixes, whitespace) as first contributions; focus on substantive improvements
